question_text: Two identical permanent magnets are placed with their north poles facing each other, 5 cm apart. They repel with a certain force. If the magnets are moved so they are only 2.5 cm apart (half the distance), which of the following best describes what happens to the repulsive force?

options.label: B
options.text: The repulsive force increases by four times or more

options.label: C
options.text: The repulsive force stays the same

explanation: Magnetic force depends on the strength of the magnetic field, which decreases very rapidly with distance (following an inverse square law at KS4+, but students should understand forces get much stronger when poles are closer). When distance is halved, the field strength increases significantly more than twice—roughly by a factor of 4 or more depending on the exact geometry.
